root_text.sn45.12:1.3: Namhi kenaci upasaṅkamitabbo, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakenā”ti.
root_text.sn45.12:1.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paṭissutvā nāssudha koci bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamati, aññatra ekena piṇḍapātanīhārakena.
root_text.sn45.12:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā tassa temāsassa accayena paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn45.12:2.2: “yena svāhaṁ, bhikkhave, vihārena paṭhamābhisambuddho viharāmi, tassa padesena vihāsiṁ.
root_text.sn45.12:2.17: chando ca avūpasanto hoti, vitakko ca avūpasanto hoti, saññā ca avūpasantā hoti, tappaccayāpi vedayitaṁ;
root_text.sn45.12:2.18: chando ca vūpasanto hoti, vitakko ca vūpasanto hoti, saññā ca vūpasantā hoti, tappaccayāpi vedayitaṁ;
root_text.sn45.12:2.19: appattassa pattiyā atthi āyāmaṁ, tasmimpi ṭhāne anuppatte tappaccayāpi vedayitan’”ti.

translation_text.sn45.12:1.2: “Mendicants, I wish to go on retreat for three months.
translation_text.sn45.12:1.3: No-one should approach me, except for the one who brings my almsfood.”
translation_text.sn45.12:1.4: “Yes, sir,” replied those mendicants. And no-one approached him, except for the one who brought the almsfood.
translation_text.sn45.12:2.1: Then after three months had passed, the Buddha came out of retreat and addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn45.12:2.2: “Mendicants, I’ve been practicing part of the meditation I practiced when I was first awakened.
translation_text.sn45.12:2.4: there’s feeling conditioned by wrong view
translation_text.sn45.12:2.5: and by the settling of wrong view,
translation_text.sn45.12:2.7: and by the settling of right view. …
translation_text.sn45.12:2.8: There’s feeling conditioned by wrong immersion
translation_text.sn45.12:2.9: and by the settling of wrong immersion, by right immersion
translation_text.sn45.12:2.10: and by the settling of right immersion.
translation_text.sn45.12:2.12: and by the settling of desire,
translation_text.sn45.12:2.14: and by the settling of thought,
translation_text.sn45.12:2.16: and by the settling of perception.
translation_text.sn45.12:2.17: As long as desire, thought, and perception are not stilled, there is feeling conditioned by that.
translation_text.sn45.12:2.18: When desire, thought, and perception are stilled, there is feeling conditioned by that.
translation_text.sn45.12:2.19: There is effort to attain the unattained. When that state has been attained, there is also feeling conditioned by that.”
